items.title: Circle’s USDC is leaving Tether behind in the stablecoin volume race, new data from Visa shows
items.description: As Wall Street banks adopt digital currencies for faster settlements, overall trading volume spiked 63% in just a single month.

items.title: Strategy selling hundreds of millions worth of bitcoin raises question about its capital-allocation playbook
items.description: First, it was a tiny amount of bitcoin sales; then, the purchase of several thousand BTC; and finally, today, the unloading of thousands of BTC. What's happening at Strategy?

items.title: Ethereum developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
items.description: The "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, updated earlier this week, has gained support from many of the network's researchers, though several say the biggest challenge won't be deciding what to build, but how quickly it can happen.

items.title: Securitize eyes acquisitions with $400 million war chest after going public, CEO says
items.description: Following its NYSE debut, Securitize CEO Carlos Domingo said the firm wants to expand its institutional tokenization platform rather than buy competitors.

items.title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
items.description: The protocol’s SUMR token fell by over 18% after the incident.

items.title: Cantor says Strategy's recovery hinges on restoring STRC to par
items.description: Bringing Strategy's preferred shares back to $100 is key to restarting Strategy's capital engine, the bank said, with repeated management actions expected to support both preferred and common shareholders.

items.title: Bitmine added another $74 million in ether as Tom Lee bets on Clarity Act boost
items.description: The second-largest digital asset treasury increased its buying pace, while Strategy sold a chunk of its bitcoin holdings.

items.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy dramatically ups pace of bitcoin sales, raising $216 million
items.description: The company sold 3,588 bitcoin last week to replenish its dollar reserves for dividends on its preferred stock.

items.title: Bitmine announces $74M Ether buys as chair says ‘greater chances of Clarity Act passage’
items.description: Bitmine's big buy contrasts with crypto treasury peer Strategy, which reported selling millions of dollars worth of its Bitcoin holdings on Monday.

items.title: UN agency moves Stellar blockchain payment initiative beyond pilot stage
items.description: The UN Development Programme said blockchain payment pilots in five countries cut costs, improved resilience and will now inform wider use across its humanitarian and development programs.

items.title: TeraWulf shares rise after $19B Anthropic AI lease, JV sale
items.description: The Bitcoin miner signed a 20-year AI infrastructure lease with Anthropic and sold its majority stake in a separate AI data center joint venture.

items.title: Bitcoin price falls up to 4% on Strategy BTC sale as trader sees Summer 2022 repeat
items.description: Bitcoin wiped out much of its latest gains thanks to a market reaction to Strategy's 3,600 BTC sell-off, but analysis eyed a potential buy announcement in the coming days.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: FCA warns of major shakeup as AI agents meet tokenized money
items.description: The FCA’s vision for agentic AI points toward a financial system where programmable money and tokenized assets could play a much larger role.

items.title: Crypto hacks fell 47% in H1 but ecosystem is no safer: CertiK
items.description: Crypto exploits rose 59% quarter-on-quarter to $807.5 million in Q2, due partly to the KelpDAO and Drift Protocol exploits, which came at the hands of North Korean hackers.

items.title: Strategy sells 3,588 Bitcoin for $216M to fund dividends, keeps $2.55B reserve intact
items.description: Strategy sold $216 million in Bitcoin to fund its dividend payments, as wealth manager Bernstein maintained its year-end Bitcoin price target of $150,000.

items.title: Trump plugs Dell at first-ever White House opening bell, sending shares soaring
items.description: Trump rang the bell in the Oval Office to open Monday's trading as he launched investment accounts for babies born from 2025 through 2028.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.description: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

items.title: Belgium loses appeal of Balogun eligibility after Trump defends intervention with FIFA
items.description: FIFA reversed Folarin Balogun’s suspension after Trump sought a review, letting the U.S. striker play Belgium as officials objected.

items.title: Trump Accounts are live. Here's how some families plan to use them
items.description: As Trump Accounts launch, millions of parents are figuring out how the new investment accounts fit with their family's financial goals.

items.title: Here's how SpaceX's Nasdaq-100 inclusion might affect options pricing
items.description: About half-a-million SpaceX options traded by midday Monday, a little below the average since inception.

items.title: Student loan servicers begin 90-day countdown for borrowers to leave SAVE plan
items.description: Student loan servicers have begun alerting borrowers that they have 90 days to leave the Biden-era SAVE plan. Here's what comes next.

items.title: Trump defends call urging Balogun red card review: 'It wasn't a foul'
items.description: "I didn't know what the hell a red card was," said Trump, who called FIFA President Gianni Infantino about the suspension of Folarin Balogun.

items.title: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell to donate stock to Trump Accounts
items.description: President Donald Trump told CNBC's Joe Kernen last week that he thought SpaceX CEO Elon Musk would donate company stock to the program.

items.description: Kyiv feared that Moscow was pressuring its closest ally to support the war effort

items.title: Fifa’s red card scandal crosses ‘Rubicon’ on to the pitch

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=So-8rVI3ZKk! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Silver has plunged 50% from its January peak to $60. Why it could hit $130 next year.
items.description: After its dramatic plunge, silver may be experiencing a huge disconnect between its traded value and its importance as a critical material.

items.title: Who should handle your estate plan: Your lawyer or your financial adviser?
items.description: The more people who have eyeballs on your situation, the better — but at some point, you are going to need a legal specialist.

items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. But that could sink your retirement plans.

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Honeywell spin-off targets AI supply chain with $14.5bn deal for Element
items.description: Deal will create a leading advanced materials company with a combined enterprise value of roughly $29bn

items.title: Toronto nude spot emerges as unlikely flashpoint over Canada investment
items.description: Airport expansion crucial to $1tn foreign investment plan threatens the birthplace of country’s gay pride movement

items.title: Hedge fund run by ex-OpenAI researcher bets on SK Hynix’s US IPO
items.description: Situational Awareness joins UK investor Baillie Gifford backing the South Korean memory maker’s American debut

items.title: Infantino defends Fifa integrity after Trump intervention over banned player
items.description: Governing body under scrutiny after US president admits asking for review into one-match ban given to Folarin Balogun

items.title: Fifa’s red card scandal crosses ‘Rubicon’ on to the pitch
items.description: The football body was once riven by bribery and corruption claims. Now it faces accusations of interference in the game itself

items.title: The next frontier for defence companies is the deep blue sea
items.description: Countries have a whole host of relatively new vulnerabilities lying on the seabed

items.title: Germany to borrow €800bn for rearmament in historic shift
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s debt-fuelled spending not seen since German reunification

items.title: The UK could help end Sudan’s civil war
items.description: The British government has leverage and should use it to proscribe a terrorist militia and cut off its supply lines

items.title: Alibaba’s A.I. Is a Hit, but Hard to Turn Into a Moneymaker
items.description: The Chinese company’s models have won over developers worldwide, but they are open source — so they can be used and modified freely.

items.title: Inside Bath & Body Works’ Fragrance Factory
items.description: “Japanese Cherry Blossom” has brought in $1.5 billion over the last 20 years. Can the company’s close-knit network of suppliers come up with the next big thing and improve sales?

items.title: Efforts to Help Smokers Quit Stall Under Trump
items.description: The C.D.C.’s tobacco control office has been shut for more than a year. After its most prominent antismoking campaign went off the air, calls to 1-800-QUIT-NOW lines have plummeted.

items.title: Gas Stations Gain When Oil Prices Start to Drop
items.description: President Trump is berating gasoline retailers for keeping prices high. Evidence suggests the business has grown more profitable.

items.title: Trump’s World Cup Intervention Roils the Soccer World, and Beyond
items.description: The U.S.-Belgium soccer game is turning out to be a huge deal even before it kicks off on Monday, thrusting politics into the spotlight.

items.title: Ships Are Moving In and Out of the Persian Gulf, Easing Oil Prices
items.description: A recovery in oil flows from the Persian Gulf and a pledge by OPEC Plus to pump more crude have put downward pressure on energy prices.

items.title: Comcast Adds Heft With Sky’s Deal for British Broadcaster ITV
items.description: The $2.1 billion purchase would combine two major British media outlets and help the Comcast-owned Sky compete with streaming giants.

items.title: Zyn’s Popularity Has Tobacco Companies Racing to Cash In
items.description: The makers of nicotine pouches are building new plants and expanding to meet demand. While influencers claim health benefits, experts warn the products can be highly addictive.
